source.title: Tracking thoughts: Exploring the neural architecture of mental time travel during mind-wandering

confidence_rationale: The study supports ARC-022's claim that the DMN and hippocampus are coupled in future-oriented simulation: stronger hippocampal-DMN connectivity predicts greater capacity for mental time travel (imagining distant times and places). This is partial empirical grounding for ARC-022's assertion that the DMN-regime and hippocampus interact in sequence generation and scenario simulation. However, the mapping fidelity is limited: the paper treats DMN and hippocampus as jointly supporting mental time travel without resolving which layer inputs to which, and does not address the typed-object pipeline structure that is ARC-022's core claim. The study is also closer to supporting ARC-014 (DMN as operating mode) than ARC-022 (DMN as pipeline layer), reinforcing the tension that ARC-022's conflict notice identifies. Confidence is moderate-low: the finding is real but addresses only the DMN-hippocampus coupling component, not the full E1->E2->DMN->Goal/Avoid->Hippocampus abstraction chain.
failure_signatures: The study supports DMN-hippocampus coupling during future simulation but does not characterise which layer sends typed input to which -- the directionality and typed-object nature of the pipeline are not tested.
The mind-wandering context conflates spontaneous future simulation with planned goal-directed simulation; ARC-022 is specifically about goal-directed planning pipelines.

The ARC-022 conflict (DMN as mode vs. pipeline layer) is not resolved by this study -- DMN-hippocampus coupling is consistent with ARC-014 (DMN operating mode activating hippocampal rollouts) as much as with ARC-022 (DMN as sequential pipeline stage).

Multimodal imaging study (structural + functional) in a developmental/adolescent-adult sample; individual difference methodology does not allow inference about the mechanistic direction of the pipeline.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Right hippocampal connectivity with default mode network regions predicts individual capacity for mental time travel during mind-wandering. Hippocampal integration with cortical regions provides the neurocognitive landscape for imagining distant times and places. Increased white-matter anisotropy in temporo-limbic tracts supports this coupling.
mapping.ree_translation: The hippocampal-DMN coupling established here partially grounds ARC-022's claim that the DMN-regime feeds into hippocampal trajectory generation. Hippocampus is not generating future simulations from raw input; it is coupled with the DMN network, consistent with ARC-022's claim that hippocampus operates on narrative/simulation objects rather than low-level sensory streams. However, the study does not establish that DMN outputs typed objects to hippocampus in a pipeline sense; it may equally support the interpretation that DMN mode-state activates hippocampal replay (consistent with ARC-014).
mapping.mapping_caveat: This paper partially maps to ARC-022 and partially to ARC-014; it is categorised as mixed evidence because it cannot discriminate between DMN-as-layer (ARC-022) and DMN-as-mode (ARC-014). The conflict at the centre of ARC-022 is not resolved.
mapping.source_context: fMRI + DTI study of adolescents and adults; individual differences in mind-wandering content (past vs. future vs. other mental). Published as a full empirical study with multivariate functional connectivity analysis.

confidence_components.notes: NeuroImage is a solid neuroimaging venue. Mapping fidelity penalised because the study supports DMN-hippocampus coupling but cannot address the typed-object pipeline directionality or resolve the DMN-as-layer vs. DMN-as-mode conflict. Transfer risk moderate: the mind-wandering context is not identical to goal-directed planning.
